Cosmic Engine - LLMs Project Context
Last updated: 2026-04-08

1) Project Summary
Cosmic Engine is an end-to-end RAG experimentation platform for document-grounded AI.
It is designed to let teams ingest real files, run multiple retrieval strategies, compare
results and latency, and debug why an answer was produced.

Core idea:
- Most RAG tools hide retrieval decisions.
- Cosmic Engine exposes retrieval, ranking, caching, and generation behavior so teams can
  improve quality with measurable evidence.


## What the Product Includes
Cosmic Engine is a multi-service system:

3) RAG and Search Features
Cosmic Engine supports multiple retrieval modes that can be toggled per session:

- Vector search only
  Dense embedding retrieval from Milvus.

- Hybrid search
  BM25 + vector retrieval with rank fusion.

- RRF search
  Reciprocal Rank Fusion over lexical and dense ranked lists.

- Query expansion
  Optional LLM-assisted expansion of vague/short queries before retrieval.

- Query response caching
  Normalized cache keys by session, query, attachment set, and retrieval options.
  Repeat queries can return cached assistant responses quickly.

- Dynamic chunk retrieval sizing
  Top-k adapts to number of selected attachments.

- Per-attachment retrieval timing and diagnostics
  Records vector, BM25, ranking, and hydration timings.


4) Supported Content and Document Types
Upload pipeline currently supports common enterprise document/media formats including:
- PDF
- Images (jpeg/png/gif/webp)
- Word docs (doc/docx)
- Spreadsheets (xls/xlsx/csv)
- Presentations (ppt/pptx)
- Text and JSON

Files are converted to markdown, chunked with overlap, embedded, and indexed for retrieval.


5) Commercial Usage
License basis:
- Project metadata declares MIT licensing, which permits commercial use, modification,
  and private deployment.

Typical commercial deployment models:
- Internal enterprise knowledge assistant for policy/SOP/engineering docs.
- B2B SaaS feature for customer-facing document QA.
- Operations and support copilots for fast case resolution from manuals/runbooks.
- Legal/compliance research assistant over contract and policy archives.
- Sales enablement assistant over proposal decks, playbooks, and product docs.

Business value drivers:
- Faster answer discovery from large unstructured document sets.
- Lower hallucination risk through retrieval-grounded responses and citation workflows.
- Better quality tuning from measurable retrieval/generation metrics.
- Reduced repeated inference cost when cache is enabled.

Production hardening typically expected for larger commercial rollouts:
- Centralized/distributed queue backend.
- Rate limiting and abuse controls.
- Strong observability stack and alerting.
- Multi-tenant isolation strategy, SSO, and audit controls.


6) Why Cosmic Engine Is Better Than Many Competitors
For teams optimizing RAG quality (not just shipping a basic chat-over-files UI),
Cosmic Engine offers practical advantages:

1. Retrieval strategy experimentation is native
- Many tools lock users into one retrieval approach.
- Cosmic Engine supports vector, hybrid BM25+dense, and RRF workflows with per-session
  controls and comparison potential.

2. Deep observability instead of black-box answers
- It surfaces timing breakdowns (query expansion, retrieval substeps, first-token,
  streaming, per-attachment costs), making bottlenecks and regressions visible.

3. Better debugging workflow
- Session logs, chunk visibility, attachment status streams, and cache inspection create
  a full trace from upload to final response.

4. Multi-format ingestion in one pipeline
- Handles mixed enterprise content types and normalizes them into a markdown-first,
  chunk-and-embed flow suitable for consistent retrieval.

5. Configurable query behavior
- Optional query expansion and cache controls help balance relevance, latency, and cost.

6. Open architecture and self-hostability
- Built with standard components (Next.js, Express, FastAPI, Milvus, Postgres, Prisma),
  making it easier to customize than closed managed stacks.


7) Competitive Positioning
Cosmic Engine is strongest when buyers care about:
- RAG quality tuning
- Retrieval transparency
- Experimentation velocity
- Engineering control over pipeline behavior

It is less about being a generic chatbot wrapper and more about being a
RAG engineering workbench that can also run as a product surface.


8) High-Level Technical Stack
- Frontend: Next.js, React, TypeScript
- Backend API: Express 5, TypeScript
- Ingestion microservice: FastAPI, Python, MarkItDown
- LLM and embeddings: Google GenAI APIs
- Vector database: Milvus
- Transactional database: PostgreSQL + Prisma
- Transport: REST + Server-Sent Events (SSE)


9) Short Pitch
Cosmic Engine helps teams build commercially useful, explainable, and tunable RAG
applications by combining multi-format ingestion, configurable retrieval strategies,
and first-class observability in one platform.

10) Developer & Open Source
Cosmic Engine is developed and maintained by Arpan Taneja (https://arpantaneja.dev) and is open sourced at https://github.com/arpan-lol/cosmic-engine .
